name: Dark Angel Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Egg whites; from 11-12 eggs, slightly below room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Resift cake flour with cocoa powder, 3/4 cup sugar, and salt.
3. In a large bowl, beat egg whites on low speed until frothy (about 1 1/2 minutes).
4. Sprinkle cream of tartar over the egg whites.
5. Beat on medium speed until white and foamy.
6. Slowly add remaining sugar, beating until soft, not stiff, with smooth peaks forming (2 to 3 minutes).
7. Add one-third of the cocoa mixture and vanilla to the egg whites. Fold gently using a rubber spatula, keeping it under the surface of the batter.
8. Repeat two more times with the remaining cocoa mixture, folding gently without overmixing.
9. Pour batter into an ungreased 9 or 10-inch tube pan.
10. Bake in the lower third of the oven for about 50 to 60 minutes, or until the cake springs back when touched and a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Remove from oven and invert the tube pan on a counter. Let cool in the pan for about 2 hours before removing.
